title: Hedyotis beddomei
intro: Hedyotis beddomei, also known as Beddome's button vine or Malay button vine, is a plant species in the family Rubiaceae, commonly found in tropical regions of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Hedyotis beddomei include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Hedyotis beddomei is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various ailments, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential interactions.
taxonomy: Hedyotis beddomei is classified under the family Rubiaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants commonly known as the madder family. The genus Hedyotis belongs to the tribe Rubieae and is primarily native to tropical and subtropical regions of Asia. This plant is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, and its classification reflects its close relationship with other members of the Rubieae tribe. Hedyotis beddomei is a species of flowering plant and is one of the many species within the Hedyotis genus. The classification of Hedyotis beddomei is based on its botanical characteristics, including its leaf shape, flower structure, and growth habit.
